task_completion.visual_evidence: The VeraCrypt main application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open the Keyfile Generator dialog from the Tools menu
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'VeraCrypt - Keyfile Generator' dialog window is visible, indicating it was selected from the Tools menu.

task_completion.description: Use the random pool to generate the keyfile
task_completion.visual_evidence: Mouse cursor moving within the Keyfile Generator window to collect random data, or the random pool indicator (progress bar or hexadecimal characters) showing activity/fullness.

task_completion.description: Set the keyfile name to 'my_keyfile.key' and target directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog or path input field showing the filename 'my_keyfile.key' and/or the path '/home/ga/Keyfiles/my_keyfile.key'.

task_completion.description: Click 'Generate and Save Keyfile' to create it
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Generate and Save Keyfile' button is clicked, or a VeraCrypt success dialog appears confirming that the keyfile has been successfully generated and saved.

integrity.description: Agent used the VeraCrypt GUI to generate the keyfile
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the VeraCrypt graphical interface rather than terminal commands (like 'dd' or 'touch') to create the file.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual VeraCrypt application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application windows match the standard VeraCrypt UI, not a text editor or a mocked interface.

integrity.description: Agent completed the actual generation process
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent spent time in the Keyfile Generator dialog using the random pool, rather than just renaming an existing file or creating a blank file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected keyfile path is exactly '/home/ga/Keyfiles/my_keyfile.key'. The expected directory is '/home/ga/Keyfiles'.
